description: You are a graphics technician for a major New York news network preparing a studio monitor for a science segment on the International Space Station and the Hubble Space Telescope. Configure GPredict for television broadcast by removing the 'Amateur' module and establishing a ground station named 'New_York_Studio' (40.7128 N, 74.0060 W, 10m altitude). Create a 'Studio_Map' module that includes only ISS (ZARYA) (NORAD 25544) and HST (Hubble Space Telescope) (NORAD 20580), assigned to the 'New_York_Studio' ground station and set to a Map-only layout. Adjust the map visuals to include the Day/Night shadow while removing the Map Grid and Sun/Moon icons. Login: username 'ga', password 'password123'.

natural_language.prompt: I need you to set up GPredict for our TV broadcast studio monitor. The default view is way too messy. First, delete the 'Amateur' module completely. Next, add a ground station for our New York studio at 40.7128 N, 74.0060 W, 10m altitude. Then, create a new module named 'Studio_Map' that tracks only the ISS (ZARYA) and the Hubble Space Telescope (HST). Make sure you bind this new module to the New York ground station, and set the layout so it only shows a single Map (no radar, no list). Finally, go into the preferences and clean up the map: turn ON the day/night shadow, but turn OFF the map grid lines and turn OFF the sun and moon icons. We want a clean, cinematic map.
